page: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DLSv5 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, featuring 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results place the D16ls_v5 in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The CPU achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1899.08 and a multi-core score of 15530.57, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 21165.21. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 839.43 GB/sec, which drops to 93.58 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In application-specific tests, the server handles 15.59 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 20.33 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, prompt processing with a 135M parameter model reaches 1024.2 tokens per second.

The primary tradeoff of the D16ls_v5 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2 and the complete absence of local storage, which necessitates external storage attachments for persistent data. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a dense allocation of compute cores relative to memory, making it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include software compilation, web serving, lightweight text and image processing, and basic database operations that fit within the 32.0 GB memory limit.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v5 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance from the DLSv5 family, running on a Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of RAM, representing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, with no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks indicate average tier capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 21165.21 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 15530.57. The instance achieves a static web serving throughput of 20.33 GB/sec and handles 15.59 million Redis SET operations per second. Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is suited for compute-bound tasks such as web serving, software compilation, and lightweight data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v5 is a compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, suited for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v5 is an x86_64 server with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, optimized for compute-bound workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual server with a low memory-to-core ratio, designed for processing-intensive workloads without local storage requirements.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
No local storage, requiring external premium storage attachments
Delivers 15.59 million Redis SET operations per second
Appropriate for web serving, software compilation, and text processing
